run.model_role_note: Primary synthesis performed in-session by Codex/GPT-5.5-equivalent role to avoid unnecessary OpenRouter spend.
run.openrouter_policy: Default Codex path makes no OpenRouter model calls; *_openrouter roles require explicit --run-openrouter.

query_strategy_scope: Identify Tier 2 butyrate quantification assays (colorimetric / enzymatic / breath-hydrogen proxy / other low-cost intermediate methods) that can be validated against Tier 3 GC-MS reference. Microbiome-derived butyrate quantification in stool, blood, breath, and culture-supernatant matrices.

query_critique.notes: PubMed coverage was adequate to reject breath proxy and generic FFA kits and to surface HPLC-UV/electrochemical candidates. Vendor/protocol search remains necessary before wet-lab adoption because commercial kit validation rarely appears in PubMed.

candidate_assays.why_candidate: Obvious low-cost kit class, but vendor protocol for Abcam ab65341 explicitly excludes acetic, propionic, and butyric acids.
required_evidence_before_verdict: Vendor protocol showing SCFA/butyrate standard curve
suggested_queries: butyric acid assay kit colorimetric GC-MS validation
free fatty acid assay kit butyric acid short chain fatty acid
likely_limitations: Medium/long-chain FFA chemistry does not transfer to SCFAs
Palmitate-standard kits can generate false confidence

candidate_assays.why_candidate: Vendor pages exist claiming butyric acid or SCFA ELISA, but the PubMed/vendor scan did not surface GC-MS validation or stool/culture matrix validation.
suggested_queries: SCFA ELISA specificity butyrate acetate propionate
likely_limitations: Small-molecule ELISA specificity may not distinguish SCFA species reliably
No PubMed validation surfaced

red_flags: Commercial kit search returns many FFA/ketone false positives, not true butyrate quantification.
trajectory_outputs.trajectory_notes: No plug-and-play colorimetric butyrate kit cleared the evidence bar.

candidate_assays.assay_name: HPLC-UV SCFA + lactate assay for bacterial culture supernatants
candidate_assays.why_candidate: PMID 23542733 reports HPLC-UV quantification of four SCFAs plus lactic acid in in vitro fermentation supernatants, with matrix-matched calibration range 0.5-50 mM and correlation/goodness-of-fit metrics in abstract.
required_evidence_before_verdict: Cross-check vs GC-MS or internal GC-MS validation on OE samples
suggested_queries: HPLC UV short chain fatty acids fermentation supernatant butyrate validation
PMID 23542733 butyrate HPLC UV full text
likely_limitations: Requires HPLC, not home assay
Culture-supernatant focused; stool and serum matrix not established
UV at 210 nm is non-selective

candidate_assays.assay_name: Thermally induced rapid esterification + VFA quantification
candidate_assays.why_candidate: PMID 28649044 reports reliable VFA quantification in aqueous phase and states derivatization/VFAME approach is more reliable than conventional colorimetric method.
required_evidence_before_verdict: Determine whether readout still requires GC
suggested_queries: volatile fatty acid methyl ester rapid esterification butyrate quantification aqueous phase
likely_limitations: Likely not Tier 2 if GC instrumentation remains required
Aqueous environmental matrix, not stool/serum

red_flags: HPLC/derivatization methods are cheaper than GC-MS only in some settings; they are not home/field assays.
trajectory_outputs.trajectory_notes: Best near-term lab candidate is HPLC-UV for culture supernatants, not stool/home testing.

candidate_assays.why_candidate: Breath hydrogen/methane papers support these gases as markers of colonic fermentation, but not as butyrate-specific quantitative measures.
required_evidence_before_verdict: Direct correlation of breath H2/CH4 with stool or serum butyrate across interventions
suggested_queries: lactulose breath test butyrate short chain fatty acid correlation
likely_limitations: Hydrogen/methane reflect total fermentation and transit/microbiome gas handling
Methane non-producer status confounds signal
Not species-specific for acetate/propionate/butyrate

candidate_assays.why_candidate: PMID 41082646 tested SCFAs in exhaled breath condensate using validated GC-MS and found oral SCFA-induced serum increases were not observed in EBC, arguing against EBC as systemic/gut butyrate proxy.
required_evidence_before_verdict: Full text confirms lack of correlation and matrix handling
suggested_queries: exhaled breath condensate short chain fatty acids serum butyrate correlation
likely_limitations: Respiratory matrix may reflect local lung microbiota more than gut fermentation

red_flags: Breath can be a fermentation/adherence proxy, not a butyrate quantification assay.
trajectory_outputs.trajectory_notes: Breath path should not be used as Tier 2 butyrate quantification.

candidate_assays.assay_name: Electrochemical fecal SCFA profiling with ANN deconvolution
candidate_assays.why_candidate: PMID 42041444 reports rapid electrochemical profiling of fecal SCFAs using esterification/dissociation fingerprints and ANN-based signal decoupling, including butyrate prediction as an auxiliary input.
required_evidence_before_verdict: Full-text performance metrics for butyrate specifically
External validation in independent fecal samples
suggested_queries: Rapid Electrochemical Profiling Fecal Short-Chain Fatty Acids butyrate ANN validation
PMID 42041444 butyrate fecal electrochemical full text
likely_limitations: Emerging research platform, not commercial kit
ML calibration may not transfer across stool matrices without retraining
Needs GC-MS cross-validation before OE adoption

candidate_assays.why_candidate: Search returns BHB/3-HB sensors, but these quantify ketone bodies, not microbial butyrate.
required_evidence_before_verdict: None; reject as wrong analyte unless chemically re-engineered
likely_limitations: Analyte mismatch

red_flags: BHB / 3-hydroxybutyrate results are false positives for this scope.
trajectory_outputs.trajectory_notes: Electrochemical fecal SCFA profiling is the most interesting future Tier 2 direction.

candidate_assays.assay_name: Culture-supernatant HPLC-UV as OE Tier 2-lab assay
candidate_assays.why_candidate: For engineered LBP / in vitro fermentation work, mM-range culture supernatants match the HPLC-UV method range better than serum/stool matrices.
required_evidence_before_verdict: OE spike-recovery with sodium butyrate in culture medium
suggested_queries: butyrate culture supernatant HPLC UV validation 0.5 50 mM
likely_limitations: Not user-facing or home assay
Instrument access required

candidate_assays.assay_name: Outsourced GC-MS / GC-MS kit as Tier 3 anchor
candidate_assays.why_candidate: Not a Tier 2 solution, but the search confirms GC-MS remains the reference anchor; Sigma/Shimadzu-style workflows and services exist for SCFA panels.
suggested_queries: SCFA GC-MS service stool butyrate culture supernatant
Short Chain Fatty Acids Kit GC-MS Sigma
likely_limitations: Does not close the low-cost/home gap
Useful as validation anchor only

red_flags: The platform gap is not one assay; it splits by matrix: culture is easier than stool/serum.
trajectory_outputs.trajectory_notes: Adopt a two-step path: HPLC-UV/electrochemical candidates as Tier 2 candidates, GC-MS as validation anchor.

consensus_candidates.assay_name: HPLC-UV SCFA + lactate assay for bacterial culture supernatants
consensus_candidates.note: Surfaced strongly in analytical/matrix-fit trajectories but below the formal 3/5 threshold because only one trajectory focused on culture-supernatant lab methods.

consensus_candidates.assay_name: Electrochemical fecal SCFA profiling with ANN deconvolution
consensus_candidates.note: Most promising stool-specific Tier 2 direction, but emerging and not ready-to-adopt.
Obvious kit class, but vendor protocol excludes SCFAs including butyric acid.

ranked_candidates.assay_name: HPLC-UV SCFA + lactate assay for bacterial culture supernatants
ranked_candidates.rationale: Best near-term assay for OE culture-supernatant / engineered-strain work. Abstract reports mM-range calibration compatible with fermentation supernatants, but it is not stool/home and needs full-text method extraction plus paired GC-MS validation.

ranked_candidates.assay_name: Electrochemical fecal SCFA profiling with ANN deconvolution
ranked_candidates.rationale: Most promising stool-specific Tier 2 direction from the PubMed snapshot. It appears designed for fecal SCFA profiling and includes butyrate, but it is a 2026 research platform requiring full-text review and independent validation before adoption.
Vendor pages exist, but no PubMed/GC-MS validation surfaced and small-molecule SCFA specificity is a major concern. Treat as false-positive-prone until protocol and spike-recovery data exist.
Useful as broad colonic-fermentation or transit/adherence proxy, but not butyrate-specific. EBC SCFA evidence also argues respiratory SCFA matrices do not track systemic SCFA increases.
must_verify_before_wet_lab: Only reconsider if a direct breath-gas-to-butyrate validation paper is found

ranked_candidates.rationale: Abcam ab65341, representative of this class, explicitly says the kit is not designed for acetic, propionic, or butyric acid. Do not use palmitate-standard FFA kits as butyrate assays.
must_verify_before_wet_lab: Reject unless a vendor produces a butyrate/SCFA-specific protocol and validation

verdict_rationale: A ready-to-adopt simple/home colorimetric or breath-based butyrate assay did not surface. Two plausible Tier 2 candidates exist: HPLC-UV for culture supernatants and an emerging electrochemical fecal SCFA platform. Both require full-text/protocol review and paired GC-MS validation before OE adoption.
limitations: This pass used PubMed title/abstract metadata plus targeted vendor/web checks; full text has not yet been extracted for key candidates.
Google Scholar / patents / Espacenet were not exhaustively harvested into machine-readable artifacts in this pass.
Commercial butyric-acid ELISA pages were not treated as validated evidence without protocol specificity and GC-MS comparison.
No non-English corpus pass was run because comp-038 is analytical-chemistry scoped and Stage 1 was not sparse; this can be revisited if needed.
Consensus threshold was interpreted qualitatively because the five trajectories were performed by Codex in-session rather than independent paid model calls.
recommended_next_step: Do a focused full-text/protocol verification pass on PMID 23542733 and PMID 42041444, plus vendor protocol audit for any butyric-acid/SCFA ELISA claims. Design a small paired validation: sodium-butyrate spike/recovery and 10-20 real samples measured by candidate Tier 2 method plus GC-MS.

evidence_sources.title: HPLC-UV method for four SCFAs and lactic acid in in vitro fermentation
Rapid electrochemical profiling of fecal SCFAs
EBC SCFAs do not track systemic SCFA increases
Free Fatty Acid Assay Kit excludes acetic, propionic, butyric acid
